ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry exists for martabak manis (Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake). FOODON_00002683 (pancake food product) used as closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Pancakes) used as closest parent; no specific martabak manis entry exists in FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for martabak manis or Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving consists of 3 fun-sized pieces of martabak manis, weighing approximately 150 g in total, as described by the vendor.

nutrient_source.title: Pancake, plain, prepared from recipe — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18263); adapted with cheese and chocolate filling components from USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for martabak manis (Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake with chocolate and cheese) exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for plain pancake (NDB 18263) as the base batter, with proportional adjustments for the chocolate sprinkle and cheddar cheese filling typical of a standard martabak manis recipe. Energy and macros are cross-checked against published Indonesian food composition references (Tabel Komposisi Pangan Indonesia, TKPI 2017, Ministry of Health Indonesia) for martabak manis. Values represent a best-estimate per 100 g of the finished product including filling.

gi_source.title: Pancake, made from packet mix — GI value 67 (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, 2008, International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for martabak manis. GI of 67 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for pancake (made from packet mix), which is the closest analogous food: a wheat-flour-based, egg-and-milk batter pancake with moderate sugar content. The chocolate and cheese filling is not expected to substantially raise the GI above this value; cheese has negligible GI and chocolate sprinkles contribute modestly. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (67 x 64.5) / 100 = 43.2, rounded to 43.1. Serving carbohydrate = 43.0 g per 100 g x 150 g serving / 100 = 64.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (280 mg per 100 g; approximately 420 mg per 150 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit martabak manis to occasional consumption, as a 150 g serving contributes approximately 420 mg sodium — about 18% of the 2,300 mg daily limit — and the high refined-carbohydrate and saturated-fat content may further stress cardiovascular regulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (GL ~43 per serving) driven by refined wheat flour batter and added sugar filling
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat martabak manis as a high-glycemic-load food; a single 150 g serving delivers approximately 64.5 g of carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~43, which is likely to cause a significant postprandial blood glucose rise and should be consumed only in small portions with careful carbohydrate budgeting.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (6.8 g per 100 g) and trans fat (0.2 g per 100 g) from butter and cheese filling
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit martabak manis due to its saturated fat content (~10.2 g per 150 g serving) and the presence of trans fat from butter, both of which are associated with elevated LDL cholesterol; occasional small-portion consumption is preferable to regular intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Martabak manis (also known as terang bulan) is a popular Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake made from a thick, yeast-leavened wheat-flour batter cooked in a round cast-iron mould until the base is golden and the interior is soft and spongy. The Original Choco Cheese variant is filled with a generous layer of chocolate sprinkles and grated or sliced cheddar cheese, then folded in half and sliced into portions. This fun-sized serving of 3 pieces weighs approximately 150 g. The texture is characteristically soft and slightly chewy on the inside with a lightly crisp base, and the flavour profile combines the mild sweetness of the batter with the richness of melted cheese and the bittersweet notes of chocolate. Martabak manis is widely sold as a street food and takeaway dessert across Indonesia and in Indonesian restaurants in Singapore. Nutritionally, it is a calorie-dense dessert providing approximately 310 kcal per 100 g, with a significant carbohydrate load (43 g per 100 g), moderate fat (12.5 g per 100 g including 6.8 g saturated fat), and moderate protein (7.2 g per 100 g) from eggs, milk, and cheese. The high sugar content (18.5 g per 100 g) and medium-to-high glycemic index (~67) make it a high-glycemic-load food per serving, which is clinically relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The sodium content (~280 mg per 100 g) is moderate and relevant for hypertension management. It contains gluten, dairy, and eggs and is not suitable for vegans or those with these allergens.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for martabak manis exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy plain pancake data with proportional adjustments for chocolate and cheese filling, cross-referenced against TKPI 2017 (Indonesian food composition table). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using the closest pancake entry. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated; no exact approved-source entry for martabak manis with chocolate-cheese filling was found in the primary regional databases. Values are derived from USDA SR Legacy (plain pancake base) with filling adjustments and cross-checked against TKPI 2017. A direct laboratory analysis or HPB/FAO entry would upgrade confidence to high. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pancake entry used as the base source. 3. vitamin_e_mg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pancake entry used as the base source. 4. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pancake entry used as the base source. 5. The food_group in the input JSON was listed as 'Dairy and eggs'; this has been corrected to 'Desserts' as martabak manis is a sweet dessert pancake, with dairy and eggs as ingredients rather than the primary food group classification. 6. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (pancake, packet mix); no direct GI measurement for martabak manis was found in the approved GI sources. 7. Trans fat value of 0.2 g per 100 g is estimated from butter content in the recipe; this is a minor contributor. 8. Halal status is assumed based on typical Indonesian Muslim-majority food preparation standards and the absence of pork or alcohol ingredients; individual restaurant halal certification should be verified.
